channel.title: My New Hugo Project
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Project

item.title: Unit 3
item.description: This unit was on reflecting on our failures. This lab for Unit 3 — Reflecting on Failure was different. Not just because we were writing about failure instead of trying to fix a broken pipeline or debug a conflict but because for once failure wasn’t something to analyze and to work on rather than hiding the msitakes. It was the whole point. Our group was Group 9, and we were assigned two things;

item.title: Unit 2
item.description:
My Experience with Understanding Information-

Going into this lab, I honestly felt a mix of confusion and slight stress. The first task was installing tools like Hugo and Git, and I wasn’t completely confident about doing it on my own. Even though it was supposed to be a simple setup, I kept second guessing whether I was doing things correctly. At the same time, once I got through it (with a bit of help and checking),it honestly felt satisfying. It made me realize that sometimes the fear of starting is worse than the actual task. When I started working with The Information: A History, a Theory, a Flood, I initially thought it would be just another reading task. But it turned out to be more thought provoking content than I expected. What I found slightly difficult was focusing on what the author is saying instead of adding my own opinions. I’m used to reacting to what I read, so sticking to pure summarizing took alot of effort. One thing that stayed with me was how information has evolved from simple communication to something that can actually be measured and analyzed. I had never really thought about information in that way before.

item.title: Unit 1
item.description:
Unit-1 : Overview of Markdown and Wiki:

  It was a group activity lab session, we were given 5 tasks first 4 of them were individual. the last task which is Task 5 it was done by group effort. 
  Task 1; Understanding WIKI- 
           In this task we were asked to explore how wikis function as a collaborative platform where multiple users can create and edit content basically. Wikis maintain history, it's easy to track changes
           and any contributions.Through this I could understand that platforms like wiki which is a documentation sites rely on wiki based or coded systems to manage large information of data.
   Task 2: Practicing Markdown- Markdown is basically an easy light language used for formatting texts easily, I got to practice basic syntaxs like headings, lists, bold and Italics.
          EXAMPLE; #Heading
                   **Bold Text**
                   *Italic Text*
                   - List Item
    This task showed me how markdown simplifies formatting texts.

  Task 3: Installing Zettlr-
           I installed Zettlr which is a markdown editor used for designing for writing and organizing notes efficiently. It's easy to focus on content creation. Using Zettlr it made me understand how 
           markdown is used in real world for writing.

  Task 4:
  Type Setting Mathmatical Expressions- In this task we were asked to practice writing mathmatical expressions using LaTeX format.
                                       Examples; E= mc^2
                                       Initially, LaTeX was abit challenging for me personally, but then by watching a few videos and help from my groupmate I could figure it out. It also proves that it can 
                                       be used for writing mathmatical content.
    GROUP ACTIVITY (TASK 5)
     We were assigned readings as per Group numbers. We were Group 3: we had to read a books sub chapter 3 the books name was: The Information: A History, a Theory, a Flood by James Gleick.
      The sub chapter 3 name is - The Uncertainty in Our Writing, the Inconsistency in Our Letters. Honestly speaking at first the title sounded a bit abstract, but as I was going through the chapter,it
      became interesting to see how basic writing wasn't always as structured as we assume it to be today. 


      Discussion and Insights -
       What stood out to me the most was the idea that writing used to be inconsistent and uncertain, which made communication more complicated than it is now. Different spellings,lack of standard rules and variation in representation made it harder to preserve and transform information accurately. While discussing this in the group, I realized how much we take structured writing very granted nowadays. Tools like markdown, which we practiced in this lab actually solve a problem that existed for centuries the need for clarity and consistency. It was quite interesting to connect something very important to what we were 
       doing practically in the same lab. It made the learning feel more meaningful rather than just random task! 


       Collaboration Experience -
                                Personally I enjoyed the group discussion because it gave me a different perspectives and I could get to know what my co group mates think about it at the same time
                                on the same chapter, some  points that I overlooked were picked up by others, whcih helped me understand the topic even more clearly. 
                                at the same time putting everyone's thoughts together wasn't completely easy- we had to align our ideas and interpretations. But that process itself made the discussion 
                                even more engaging and interactive. Overall, it felt less like a formal task and more like a shared learning experience. What I found more interesting was how a simple 
                                concept like markdown connects historical changes when it comes to writing, making  the lab 1 feel more practical.
